mnemonics: CRISP: Cost Reduction, Increased Satisfaction, Reputation, Security, Predictability
keyPoints: Reduces long-term costs by minimizing rework and maintenance
Increases customer satisfaction and trust in the product
Enhances security and reliability, reducing risks and failures
commonMistakes: Focusing only on functional requirements while ignoring non-functional quality attributes like performance and security
Treating quality assurance as a final phase rather than integrating it throughout the entire development lifecycle
Equating 'no bugs found' with high quality, without considering usability, maintainability, or scalability
